title: SniperBot Crypto Eligibility / Exclusion Decision Schema
description: Pure structural contract for deterministic, descriptive, non-authorizing classification of caller-supplied Crypto eligibility, exclusion, restriction, evidence-quality, lane-confirmation, generic-context, and optional contextual authority facts for one opaque Crypto subject. References are opaque. Caller-supplied context is descriptive, non-governing, and non-operational. This schema encodes no evaluator precedence, identity resolution, token or pair selection, market interpretation, runtime filtering, integration, orchestration, exchange, broker, wallet, custody, DeFi, smart-contract, account, routing, order, execution, or live-money authority.
$comment: Founder-decision trace: Decisions 01 and 13 define the opaque crypto_reference; 02, 16, and 46 define CryptoLaneConfirmation; 03-07 define descriptive non-authorizing meanings and Deferral separation; 08-14 define Request order, requiredness, strict JSON types, closed representation, references, omission, and null; 15 prohibits identity decomposition; 17-34 and 36-37 make venue, exchange, chain, network, bridge, wrapped-asset, stablecoin, wallet, custody, DeFi, smart-contract, liquidity, volatility, market-condition, tokenomics, jurisdiction, regulation, sanctions, platform-policy, account-permission, margin, collateral, leverage, and liquidation properties non-applicable and therefore absent; 35 excludes policy collision fields; 38-45 define evidence and posture properties; 47-48 define GenericAssetClassContext; 49-53 define AuthorityEvidence; 54-56 define Outcome, ReasonCode, RequiredAction, and the emittable subset; 57-62 govern evaluator mappings, collisions, undefined states, precedence, and totality and are intentionally not encoded as schema logic; 63 defines Decision; 64-65 define this schema identity, draft, closure, and parity; 66-69 govern the future Python package, API, exceptions, purity, and imports and create no schema property; 70-72 govern later tests, exhaustive evidence, validation, provenance, acceptance, and stops and create no schema property.

AssetClass.description: Closed caller-supplied generic asset-class vocabulary. No value is inferred from a reference or external data. Only CRYPTO is contextually compatible with this subject.

CryptoLaneConfirmation.description: Closed caller-supplied Crypto-lane confirmation vocabulary. Confirmation is never inferred from the subject reference or another context.

Validity.description: Closed validity vocabulary for supplied generic context and authority evidence.

Outcome.description: Closed descriptive outcomes only. ELIGIBLE means only continued governed Stage 2 consideration and never approval, selection, support, trading permission, transfer authority, order authority, execution authority, or live-money authority.

ReasonCode.description: Exact closed 23-value Crypto Eligibility / Exclusion reason vocabulary. Aliases, unknown values, free text, Asset-Class, Options, Stock, Crypto Deferral / No-Action, venue, network, wallet, market, policy, account, risk, order, and execution reasons are not permitted.

RequiredAction.description: Full closed five-value vocabulary. FOUNDER_AUTHORITY_REQUIRED and RESET_REQUIRED are vocabulary-only and are not decision-emittable by this subject. No value authorizes operational behavior.

context_reference.description: Opaque caller-supplied generic-context reference containing at least one non-whitespace character. It is not parsed, normalized, looked up, or interpreted.

GenericAssetClassContext.description: Optional by request-property omission only. JSON null and empty objects are invalid. This context is descriptive, externally supplied, non-governing, and non-authorizing. The schema encodes no generic-context subprecedence and invokes no evaluator.

evidence_reference.description: Opaque caller-supplied authority-evidence reference containing at least one non-whitespace character. It is not parsed, normalized, looked up, or interpreted.

AuthorityEvidence.description: Optional contextual authority evidence by request-property omission only. JSON null and empty objects are invalid. All fields are required when supplied. This externally supplied structure is descriptive, non-governing, and non-operational; it grants no eligibility, permission, market access, transaction authority, order authority, execution authority, or live-money authority and encodes no authority subprecedence.

crypto_reference.description: Opaque caller-supplied Crypto subject reference containing at least one non-whitespace character. It may designate a token, coin, pair, or another normalized Crypto instrument in the caller's system, but it is not parsed, decomposed, normalized, validated, selected, ranked, recommended, looked up, or interpreted.

eligibility_evidence_current.description: Caller-supplied evidence-currentness fact. The schema performs no clock, timestamp, market-data, chain-state, or policy lookup.

eligibility_evidence_sufficient.description: Caller-supplied evidence-sufficiency fact. The schema performs no scoring, calculation, screening, or policy lookup.

eligibility_evidence_contradictory.description: Caller-supplied explicit evidence-contradiction fact. The schema infers no contradiction and encodes no collision behavior.

crypto_eligible.description: Caller-supplied descriptive Crypto eligibility posture. It is not approval, selection, support, permission, routing authority, order authority, execution authority, or live-money authority.

crypto_excluded.description: Caller-supplied descriptive Crypto exclusion posture. It is not a runtime blocklist, filter, enforcement action, routing rule, or order rejection rule.

crypto_restricted.description: Caller-supplied descriptive Crypto restriction posture. It is not a market, exchange, account, wallet, custody, transfer, order, or execution control.

eligibility_evidence_reference.description: Opaque eligibility-evidence reference containing at least one non-whitespace character. It is required even when evidence is asserted absent and is not parsed, normalized, looked up, or interpreted.

correlation_reference.description: Opaque correlation reference containing at least one non-whitespace character. Exact preservation is evaluator-owned.

crypto_lane_confirmation.description: Optional. Omission or JSON null represents missing Crypto-lane confirmation. This schema infers no confirmation from the subject or another context.

generic_asset_class_context.description: Optional by property omission only. JSON null is invalid. No generic decision object is consumed and no generic evaluator is imported, invoked, or orchestrated.

authority_evidence.description: Optional by property omission only. JSON null is invalid. Supplied evidence is contextual, descriptive, non-governing, non-operational, and non-authorizing.

Request.description: Closed request containing caller-supplied descriptive facts only. Venue, exchange, chain, network, bridge, wrapped-asset, stablecoin, wallet, custody, address, private-key, signing, deposit, withdrawal, transfer, DeFi, smart-contract, liquidity, volatility, market-condition, tokenomics, jurisdiction, regulation, sanctions, platform-policy, account, balance, margin, collateral, leverage, liquidation, risk, sizing, strategy, signal, routing, order, and execution properties are prohibited. Every approved typed posture, evidence-quality, lane, generic-context, and authority combination remains structurally representable for future evaluator classification. This schema encodes no first-match precedence, undefined-state evaluation, runtime logic, integration, orchestration, data access, networking, persistence, registration, trading, execution, or live-money behavior.

required_action.description: Exact emittable RequiredAction subset. NONE is descriptive only and never means permission. FOUNDER_AUTHORITY_REQUIRED and RESET_REQUIRED are not valid decision emissions.

crypto_reference.description: Opaque Crypto reference preserved exactly from the request; cross-object equality is evaluator-owned.

eligibility_evidence_reference.description: Opaque eligibility-evidence reference preserved exactly from the request; cross-object equality is evaluator-owned.

correlation_reference.description: Opaque correlation reference preserved exactly from the request; cross-object equality is evaluator-owned.

Decision.description: Closed descriptive result surface only. It contains no permission, authorization, identity resolution, market interpretation, selection, recommendation, strategy, signal, risk, sizing, exchange, broker, wallet, custody, account, transfer, route, order, transaction, runtime, integration, orchestration, trading, execution, or live-money field.
